rules: Ideal C:N ratio for composting: 25:1 to 35:1
By volume, roughly 3 parts brown to 1 part green

faq.question: What is the ideal compost ratio?
faq.answer: The ideal carbon-to-nitrogen (C:N) ratio is 25:1 to 35:1 by weight. In practice, this means roughly 3 parts brown material to 1 part green material by volume. This ratio creates the best conditions for decomposition.

faq.question: What are greens and browns in composting?
faq.answer: Greens (nitrogen): food scraps, coffee grounds, fresh grass, manure. Browns (carbon): dry leaves, straw, cardboard, newspaper, wood chips. Greens provide protein for microbes, browns provide energy and structure.

faq.question: Why does my compost smell?
faq.answer: Bad smell = too many greens (nitrogen), too wet, or not enough oxygen. Fix: add brown material, turn the pile for aeration, and ensure drainage. A healthy compost pile should smell earthy, not rotten.

faq.question: Is the Compost Ratio Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard gardening and horticulture formulas trusted by gardeners and growers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Compost Ratio Calculator?
sections.body: The Compost Ratio Calculator is a free online tool designed for gardeners and growers who need quick, accurate calculations in the gardening and horticulture space. By entering your green material, brown material, you get instant results including current c:n ratio, assessment, adjustment needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting current c:n ratio right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In gardening and horticulture, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by gardeners and growers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to grow a thriving garden with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your green material and need to find the right current c:n ratio. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
